question: Investigators prospectively studied 75 consecutive patients undergoing coronary atherectomy for symptomatic coronary artery disease. Before atherectomy was performed, blood levels of anticytomegalovirus (CMV) antibodies were measured. Forty-nine patients were seropositive for CMV, and 26 patients were seronegative. Subjects were then followed for 6 months after atherectomy to determine incidents of restenosis.  If we make the simplifying assumption that the sample represents a simple random sample of like patients, we can view this as a naturalistic sample of $N=75$ in which $n_1=49$ are seropositive and $n_2=26$ are seronegatives for CMV.
